reasoning: Acquiring a nuclear weapon requires significant time, infrastructure, and testing. As of November 2025, no eligible US ally (e.g., South Korea, Japan, Germany, Saudi Arabia, Turkey) has an active, overt nuclear weapons program that could realistically yield an operational, independent weapon by the end of December 2025. While there are ongoing political discussions regarding nuclear latency and deterrence, transitioning from discussions or latent capabilities to possessing an officially confirmed, operational nuclear weapon is impossible within the remaining two-month timeframe. There are no indications of a sudden technology transfer (e.g., Saudi Arabia receiving a weapon from Pakistan) taking place. Thus, the probability is extremely low and pushed to the minimum allowed bound.
batch_queries: Will a US ally get a nuke in 2025?
